hypothesis: Training the multimodal contrastive model with multiple low-energy conformers per SMILES (instead of a single canonical conformer) will induce 3D embeddings that are invariant to SO(3) rotations and small intramolecular conformational changes, yielding significantly higher multi-pose retrieval rates (≥10% absolute increase in "3 Rot. Retrieved") and lower MAE on conformer-sensitive QM9 properties (e.g., dipole moment and polarizability) compared to a model pretrained with only canonical conformers.
